feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Business Technology Platform
feed.subtitle: SAP Business Technology Platform Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP BTP and clean core.
entry.content:
Let’s discuss. 

  1. What does “Clean Core” really mean in your organisation today — zero custom code, or controlled extensibility via BTP?
  2. Where do Clean Core programs fail most often: technical design, governance, or business buy-in?
  3. Is Clean Core achievable without a strong BTP strategy — or is BTP effectively mandatory now?

entry.title: Sap BTP innovation
entry.content:
I M interested to hear from experts, what you think about these . 

1. Are teams using BTP as a strategic platform, or has it become just another integration and extension toolbox

2. Which BTP capability delivers the most real value today: Integration Suite, CAP, Event Mesh, or AI services 

3. How are you preventing BTP from becoming the new custom-code dumping ground?

entry.title: Cannot deploy CAP application in SAP BTP Trial – stuck at "Deploying the Application"
entry.content:
Hello,

I am following this SAP Learning course using a SAP BTP Trial account:

https://learning.sap.com/courses/develop-extensions-with-cap-following-the-sap-btp-developer-s-guide/deploying-the-application-1

I am currently stuck at the "Deploying the Application" step.

When I try to deploy the application, the deployment fails and does not complete successfully.

My environment:
- SAP BTP Trial account
- SAP Business Application Studio
- CAP project

I followed the tutorial steps, but I still get errors during deployment.


Is this issue related to Trial account limitations, outdated tutorial steps, or environment settings?

Could you please advise how to resolve this issue or how to troubleshoot it?

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Proper End-to-End Steps to Consume SAP Business Accelerator Hub APIs in SAP Integration Suite (OAuth
entry.content:
I’m trying to integrate SAP Integration Suite (CPI) with APIs from SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

I initially used API keys via HTTP headers, but ran into 401/403 errors, which made it unclear:

  • when API keys are sufficient, and

  • when OAuth 2.0 is mandatory.

I’m also confused about the exact BTP services and steps required to obtain OAuth client credentials for real SAP S/4HANA Cloud APIs, especially compared to sandbox APIs that seem to work with API keys only.

I’m looking for a clear end-to-end explanation of:

  • When to use API Key vs OAuth 2.0

  • How sandbox APIs behave (read-only / mock data)

  • Exact BTP steps to obtain OAuth credentials for S/4HANA Cloud APIs

  • How to configure CPI correctly to consume these APIs

entry.title: issue VSCODE connect to ABAP on BTP
entry.content:
Hi Guys, we have an abap system on BTP, its okay the connection to develop Fiori apps using BAS

We are facing issues to develop locallly using VSCODE, i have error 401 when im trying to add Sap System abap on BTP:

Okay, its authorization issue, but i dont have place to import service key here.

Any Idea how to solve it?

entry.title: How to get the security related incidents alert from BTP Applications
entry.content:
Team,

We need to know how to get the alerts from BTP applications if security related incidents happens.

Regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Can't create package in ZLOCAL in shared instance SAP BTP Free Trail
entry.content:
Ignore the question - I found solution, I just have updated my ADT tool to last version and issue is gone.

Hi, Colleagues,

I have just executed booster 'Prepare Account for ABAP Trail' and logged into system via Eclipse. I can't create subpackage under ZLOCAL.  The button 'Finish' is disabled and when I try to hit the button 'Next', I just stay on the same screen and no errors are got. 

Trail in US region:

I see others packages are adding, but I can't create. Any help, colleagues?

entry.title: Instance for Data Attribute Recommendation cannot be created
entry.content:
Hello, wanted to try out Data Attribute Recommendation in SAP Hana Trial account. In December it was working fine, but when I tried again in February, Instance creation is stuck. Creating service Data Attribute Recommendation takes a long time, about a day, after which an error occurs that the wait time has been exceeded.

Steps to recreate:

  1. Created SAP Hana trial.
  2. Created AWS US-east subaccount.
  3. Used either Booster to set up account for Data Attribute Recommendation or created service manually (tried with different Runtime Environments, and each time subaccount was deleted and created anew)
  4. After near a day long wait, error occurs about waiting time being exceeded

entry.title: Resolving Missing HDI Build Plugin in Multi-Tenant SAP HANA Deployment
entry.content:
In a multi-tenant SAP HANA Cloud application using HDI containers for tenant isolation, the error "Could not find the 'com.sap.hana.di.schedulerjob' build plugin" occurs when new release artifacts require additional DI (Data Integration) plugins not present in consumer tenant containers.[1][2][3]

Scenario
Your production multi-tenant application serves live consumer tenants, each isolated in dedicated HDI containers for security and data segregation. A recent release introduced new HANA artifacts (e.g., tables, views) needing extra build plugins like `com.sap.hana.di.schedulerjob` for scheduler job support during HDI deployment or updates via tools like `cds deploy --to hana`.

Root Cause
Consumer tenant HDI containers lack the required DI plugins and admin privileges for library configuration, blocking Retriever deployments that trigger database updates. Standard CAP multitenancy provisions containers but doesn't auto-configure release-specific plugins across all tenants.

Resolution Steps
1. Grant Script Execution Rights: As database admin (e.g., SYSTEM user), assign `EXECUTE` privileges on target consumer containers to a dedicated script user (e.g., `<NEW_CONTAINER_ADMIN>`):
```
GRANT EXECUTE ON <container_name> TO <NEW_CONTAINER_ADMIN>;
```

2. Execute Privilege and Library Setup Scripts (connect as `<NEW_CONTAINER_ADMIN>` to the target container):
```sql
CREATE LOCAL TEMPORARY COLUMN TABLE #PRIVILEGES LIKE _SYS_DI.TT_API_PRIVILEGES;
INSERT INTO #PRIVILEGES (PRINCIPAL_NAME, PRIVILEGE_NAME, OBJECT_NAME)
SELECT '<NEW_CONTAINER_ADMIN>', PRIVILEGE_NAME, OBJECT_NAME
FROM _SYS_DI.T_DEFAULT_CONTAINER_ROLE_ADMIN_PRIVILEGES;
CALL <container_name>#DI.GRANT_CONTAINER_API_PRIVILEGES(#PRIVILEGES, _SYS_DI.T_NO_PARAMETERS, ?, ?, ?);
DROP TABLE #PRIVILEGES;
CALL <container_name>#DI.CONFIGURE_LIBRARIES(_SYS_DI.T_DEFAULT_LIBRARIES, _SYS_DI.T_NO_PARAMETERS, ?, ?, ?);
```
- The `?` placeholders capture output parameters (e.g., success flags, error details) – bind variables like `VARCHAR(100)` for each.
- `_SYS_DI.T_DEFAULT_CONTAINER_ROLE_ADMIN_PRIVILEGES` provides standard admin privileges.
- `_SYS_DI.T_DEFAULT_LIBRARIES` enables core plugins including schedulerjob for build-time artifact deployment.[3]

3. Trigger Retriever Deployment: Run your update process (e.g., `cds deploy` or CI/CD pipeline) to apply new artifacts. Tenant containers now support the missing plugin.

Key Learnings for Blog
- Proactive Plugin Check: Before releases, verify required DI plugins via `CALL <container>#DI.GET_CONTAINER_INFO()` and document in CI/CD.
- Automation Opportunity: Integrate these scripts into SaaS provisioning callbacks (e.g., via SAP Service Manager) for on-subscribe upgrades.
- Best Practice: Use separate HDI containers per tenant; reuse HANA instances across apps but isolate via Service Manager instances to avoid data loss on unsubscription.
- Monitoring: Post-fix, confirm via `_SYS_DI.T_CONTAINER_LIBRARIES` that `com.sap.hana.di.schedulerjob` is active.

This manual fix ensures zero-downtime updates in live multi-tenant environments while maintaining isolation. Test in dev tenants first.

entry.title: 401 failing with SAP API hub sandbox Odata APIs
entry.content:
Hi team, 

Even after using the correct API key, I am facing a 401 not authorized response from all APIs from SAP Business API hub, 

Example 

https://api.sap.com/api/CE_WHSEPHYSICALSTOCKPRODUCTS_0001/tryout

- Request URL: 

https://sandbox.api.sap.com/s4hanacloud/sap/opu/odata4/sap/api_physicalinventorydocument/srvd_a2x/sap/physicalinventorydocument/0001/BookSerialNumber?$top=50
- response: 401

or others too:

https://api.sap.com/api/sap-s4-CE_PHYSICALINVENTORYDOCUMENT_0001-v1/tryout

or 

https://api.sap.com/api/CE_FREIGHTBOOKING_0001/tryout

I did not have any issues just last week. Is there anyone else facing the same issue? Is there any known issue with the SAP Business API hub currently?

entry.title: BTP BuildApps- Error handling and logging possibility
entry.content:
Hello community,

Currently,  we are developing Build Apps as mashup/extension for Sales and Service Cloud V2 or C4C V2.

Now we have use case to handle the address validation logic in Build Apps where the external APIs are consumed, that clearly means the APIs could give us different errors/exceptions in case any issue occurred from external system. How can we log and monitor errors occurred in Build Apps from external systems ?

Examples:
Network & Connectivity Errors: Timeouts, DNS failures, and connection resets.
Authentication/Authorization Errors: 401 Unauthorized, 403 Forbidden, invalid or expired API keys

Rate Limits and Quotas: Exceeding allowed request volume
Data Format/Schema Mismatches: API changes its data structure
API Provider Downtime (5xx Errors): 500 Internal Server Error (server crash) or 503 Service Unavailable (server overload)
Invalid URL/Endpoint Changes: The provider updates their documentation or removes an endpoint without notice

Can you guide us how to handle errors in Build Apps so that we can take advantage of logging and monitoring?
Thank you.
Willem_Pardaens @vvdries @gregorw @qmacro @martinstenzig

entry.title: Looking for SAP AI course recommendations
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

We’ve been working more closely with SAP AI capabilities lately (especially around S/4HANA and automation scenarios), and we'd love to deepen our knowledge.

For those of you already exploring AI in SAP: Which SAP AI courses from the Learning Hub (or from other SAP related platform) would you actually recommend? Any that are more practical vs. too theoretical?

entry.title: BTP Quota Assignment for Consumption Based Model : Is it a Hard Error ?
entry.content:
As we know BTP allows us to set certain BTP numerical Quota at a subaccount or Directory level.
For Consumption + Subscription model what happens if the user exceeds that quota. For example if we set certain workzone standard user quota assigned to a particular subaccount/directory but on a certain month in productive environment more users login will the user get an error or they will simply be charged for the overconsumption. 
Also does it differ service by service or it is same for all service ? Is there a way to set up alert to notify that they are about to exceed quota.

entry.title: Using Concur API in Build Proces Automation
entry.content:
Hi, 

in SAP Build Process Automation we're trying to access Concur data via API. 

We tried to run GET Retrieve Users (Identity v4 API) and stated /profile/identity/v4 as Resource Path and received this message: 

"{\"status\":400,\"schemas\":[\"urn:ietf:params:scim:api:messages:2.0:Error\",\"urn:ietf:params:scim:api:messages:concur:2.0:Error\"],\"urn:ietf:params:scim:api:messages:concur:2.0:Error\":{\"messages\":[{\"code\":\"BAD_REQUEST\"}]},\"detail\":\"Company ID is required\"}"

We're assuming that there's something missing within our destination configuration in our BTP subaccount. 
We used the following settings:

Type: HTTP
Proxy Type: Internet
URL: https://eu2.api.concursolutions.com
Authentication: OAuth2ClientCredentials
Client ID
Client Secret
Token Service URL: https://eu2.api.concursolutions.com/oauth2/v0/token
Token Service URL Type: Dedicated

Additional Properties
companyID


In Concur we generated an OAuth2 App with the Grants refresh_token, client_credentials and password and selected all available scopes.

Any hints on how to make this API work are greatly appreciated!

Thanks
Franziska

entry.title: How to configure Single Sign-On (SSO) between S/4HANA Cloud and BTP
entry.content:
I have subscribed to SAP Business Technology Platform and developed an application using SAP Business Application Studio. The application was successfully deployed to the Cloud Foundry environment.

After that, I created a custom app in SAP S/4HANA Cloud and assigned the final application URL (obtained from BAS) to the app so that the application can be launched directly from the S/4HANA Cloud system.

Currently, the application can be executed successfully from S/4HANA Cloud. However, users are required to enter BTP credentials every time they access the application. Since business users of the S/4HANA Cloud system are not able to provide BTP credentials for each execution, this approach is not convenient for them.

I found that Single Sign-On (SSO) needs to be configured between S/4HANA Cloud and BTP to avoid repeated authentication.

Could you please share any blogs, reference guides, or official documentation that explain how to configure SSO between these two systems?

Best Regards,

Khaing Zin

entry.title: Erro ao tentar ativer o ativar o SAP Build Apps (plano free)
entry.content:
Estou tentando ativar o SAP Build Apps (plano free) no subaccount trial, mas a assinatura fica em Subscription Failed.

O booster Get Started with SAP Build Apps não avança na etapa Select Scenario devido a falta de quota. 

Já criei novo subaccount e repeti o processo, o erro persiste.

 

Preciso de ajuda, por favor.

entry.title: SAP Integration suite |cloud integration | Egress IPs | Clarification needed
entry.content:
We have shared IPs from SAP documentation, but it is very wide list of IPs and they are asking to share specific set of IPs from where traffic will be initiated in cloud integration. Can you please suggest if there is possibility to limit the Egress Ips for SFTP based scenarios.

There was also a question whether these Ips are used for any other customer or its dedicated to our company. Can you please confirm ?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Expose Joule Studio Agent/Skills as API for UI-triggered intelligent insights (without chatbot inter
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I’m currently exploring SAP Joule Studio capabilities and wanted to understand if it’s possible to expose Joule Studio agents or skills as an API/service so they can be triggered programmatically from a UI rather than through the Joule chatbot interface.

Use Case:
In our application UI, users interact with specific components (for example, clicking a marker or icon on a screen). When the user clicks this marker, we want to:

  1. Immediately trigger a backend call.

  2. Invoke a Joule Studio Agent or Skill that generates intelligent insights based on the context/data of that UI component.

  3. Return the generated insight and display it directly in a UI card or insight panel.

So instead of the user asking a question through the chatbot prompt, the insight is automatically generated and displayed on the UI based on the user action.

What I want to confirm:

  • Is it possible to expose Joule Studio agents/skills as an API endpoint that can be invoked programmatically?

  • Can Joule-generated insights be returned as structured output suitable for embedding in a custom UI component/card?

  • Are there recommended patterns or SAP services (e.g., AI Core, AI Launchpad, CAP services, etc.) that should be used to orchestrate this?

  • If Joule Studio itself cannot be directly exposed as an API, what would be the recommended architecture to achieve this UI-driven intelligent insight pattern?

The goal is to enable context-aware insights triggered directly from UI interactions, rather than requiring users to manually prompt a chatbot.

Any guidance, architecture suggestions, or documentation references would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: While configuring the SAp BUild Apps i am getting below error.
entry.content: Couldn't subscribe to Cloud Identity Services. Try again. If the problem persists, please post a question to SAP Community.

entry.title: @sap/cap-rfc update to use a RFC websocket destination
entry.content: Will there be an update to the @sap/cap-rfc library to use a destination from the btp destination service, that is configured as RFC Websocket, since the Cloud Connector 2.19 allows such connections? This would make it easier for applications to use secured rfc connections with principal propagation without configuring SNC.
